message:
fix(connector_governor): rejected quota attempts must not increment total_lifetime

QuotaTracker::record previously incremented `self.total_actions` BEFORE
the quota-exhaustion check:

    pub fn record(&mut self, now_ms: u64) -> bool {
        self.gc(now_ms);
        self.total_actions += 1;              // <- bug
        if self.window_actions.len() as u64 >= self.config.max_actions {
            return false;
        }
        self.window_actions.push(now_ms);
        true
    }

Result: the `total_lifetime` counter exposed via `QuotaSnapshot`
reflected every *attempt*, including rejected ones — not every
*allowed* action, which is what the field name and the downstream
governor snapshot consumers assume. A connector in a tight retry
loop after hitting its quota would see `total_lifetime` march up
even though no action actually fired.

Fix: move the increment to AFTER the exhaustion check so
`total_actions` only counts allowed actions. `window_actions` already
had the correct ordering (push only on the allow path); just needed
to move `total_actions += 1` alongside it.

    pub fn record(&mut self, now_ms: u64) -> bool {
        self.gc(now_ms);
        if self.window_actions.len() as u64 >= self.config.max_actions {
            return false;
        }
        self.window_actions.push(now_ms);
        self.total_actions += 1;              // <- moved
        true
    }

## Two regression tests

1. `quota_rejected_attempts_do_not_increment_total_lifetime`
   Narrow unit pin: QuotaTracker directly, max_actions=2,
   window_ms=10_000. Two allows + one reject. Asserts
   total_actions() == 2 and full QuotaSnapshot shape with
   total_lifetime=2, used=2, remaining=0, usage_fraction=1.0.

2. `governor_snapshot_connector_quota_lifetime_excludes_rejected_attempts`
   End-to-end through ConnectorGovernor: configure a default_quota
   with max_actions=1, evaluate an action that's allowed, evaluate a
   second action that's rejected with GovernorReason::ConnectorQuotaExhausted,
   then assert the snapshot()'s slack connector entry has
   quota.total_lifetime=1 (not 2), used=1, remaining=0.

The second test is the one that matters for the observable bug —
the rejected-attempts contamination only shows up once the governor
aggregates per-connector quotas into a snapshot that operators read.
The first test pins the QuotaTracker invariant directly so any
future refactor that moves the increment back up is caught at the
narrowest surface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
